response.justification: The candidate has a broad and lengthy full-stack software engineering career (16+ years) with some exposure to high-performance systems and recent AI agent tooling, but possesses none of the domain-specific qualifications required for this role. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, hardware architecture design, GPU workload analysis, SIMD/MIMD programming, or deep learning inference/training optimization — the core competencies the Principal Performance Architect position demands. The work at Sociomantic (low-latency micro-services in DLang) is the closest analog to performance engineering, but it is network I/O performance, not hardware/silicon-level optimization for AI accelerators.
strengths: Meets minimum education/experience threshold (BS + 16+ years of professional engineering experience)
Demonstrated high-performance systems work at Sociomantic: low-latency async I/O, fiber concurrency, and high-throughput HTTP pipelines in DLang
Recent exposure to AI infrastructure at re:cinq (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) shows awareness of the AI tooling ecosystem
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind — the primary technical responsibility of this role is entirely absent from the resume
Zero GPU, SIMD, or MIMD architecture background; all preferred hardware qualifications are unmet
AI experience is limited to application-layer agent tooling; no deep learning workload optimization, training pipelines, or inference performance work is present
